semantic.role: Vitest test suite for validating cross-process lock acquisition and release behaviors using an isolated worker script.
responsibilities: Spawns child processes running the lock worker script with configurable operation modes (acquire-release, acquire-hold, acquire-release-rewrite).
Listens to worker stdout and stderr to capture and parse JSON results for assertion.
Cleans up temporary directories and processes after each test to ensure isolation.
Asserts expected lock payload serialization/parsing consistency across process boundaries.
Validates error codes and messages for failure scenarios in multi-process lock operations.
outOfScope: Implementing the core lock acquisition logic (delegated to the worker script).
Handling real-time lock coordination beyond test simulation.
invariants: Uses Vitest testing framework with describe/it/beforeEach/afterEach structure.
Relies on Node.js child_process for process isolation in tests.
Depends on lock payload serialization utilities from src/utils/lock.js.
Cleans up test artifacts via FileSystemManager.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Test suite for validating lock behavior in multi-process scenarios, ensuring isolation and serialization consistency.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Refactoring to establish subsystem facades and resolve layer inversions may have impacted test dependencies or structure, but no direct evidence in this file snippet.

semantic.driftReason: Previous semantic contract described the worker script itself (lock-worker.mjs), but the analyzed file is the test suite that uses that worker. The role shifted from 'worker script' to 'test suite for worker'.

responsibilities: Spawns child processes running the lock worker script with configurable operation modes such as acquire-release, acquire-hold, and acquire-release-rewrite.
Listens to worker stdout and stderr to capture and parse JSON results for assertion.
Cleans up temporary directories and processes after each test to ensure isolation.
Asserts expected lock payload serialization and parsing consistency across process boundaries.
Validates error codes and messages for failure scenarios in multi-process lock operations.
outOfScope: Implementing the core lock acquisition logic, which is delegated to the worker script.
Handling real-time lock coordination beyond test simulation.
